title: Removing Contiguous Duplicates from Rotated Array

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers `nums` where each element appears `n` times and `n` is not equal to 1, remove all duplicate elements in `nums` such that each element appears only once in the resulting array. You can rotate the array to make elements appear first in their groups. The modified array should contain all the elements of `nums` in their original order.
statement.inputFormat: The input will consist of a single line containing the elements of the array, space-separated. The total number of elements in the array will be between 1 and 10,000, inclusive.
statement.outputFormat: Output the elements of the resulting array, space-separated in the same order as they appear in the input.
statement.notes: The resulting array can have fewer elements than the original array. The input guarantee that each element appears `n` times in `nums`.

examples.explanation: In this example, 1, 2, 3, and 4 are the only elements that are not in any group of three consecutive elements.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> solve(vector<int>& nums); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int[] solve(int[] nums); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> list[int]: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> solve(vector<int>& nums) { vector<int> result = {nums[0]}; for (int i = 1; i < nums.size(); i++) { if (nums[i] != nums[i - 1]) { result.push_back(nums[i]); } } return result; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int[] solve(int[] nums) { int[] result = new int[] {nums[0]}; for (int i = 1; i < nums.length; i++) { if (nums[i] != nums[i - 1]) { result = addElement(result, nums[i]); } } return result; } private int[] addElement(int[] array, int value) { int[] newArray = new int[array.length + 1]; System.arraycopy(array, 0, newArray, 0, array.length); newArray[newArray.length - 1] = value; return newArray; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> list[int]: result = [nums[0]] for i in range(1, len(nums)): if nums[i] != nums[i - 1]: result.append(nums[i]) return result
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(nums) { var result = [nums[0]]; for (var i = 1; i < nums.length; i++) { if (nums[i] != nums[i - 1]) { result.push(nums[i]); } } return result; }

solutions.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> list[int]: result = {} for num in nums: result[num] = True return sorted(result.keys())
solutions.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> solve(vector<int>& nums) { unordered_map<int, bool> result; for (int num : nums) result[num] = true; vector<int> answer; for (auto& pair : result) answer.push_back(pair.first); return answer; } };
